name: Parque de Polvoranca y la Laguna de María Pascuala – running circular
region_intro: Jogging around Leganés offers a variety of routes through its urban green spaces and natural parks. The region is characterized by relatively flat terrain, featuring several parks and lakes that provide pleasant running environments. These areas are ideal for those seeking easy jogging routes Leganés, as well as longer distance running trails near Leganés. Many routes are circular jogging routes Leganés, making them convenient for local runners. Best jogging routes around Leganés The most popular jogging route is Parque de Polvoranca y la Laguna de María Pascuala – running circular, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes about 40 minutes to complete. This circular route explores a park and its lake, offering scenic jogging routes Leganés views. Another top favourite among local runners is Lake in Butarque Park – Las Presillas Park loop from San Nicasio, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.1 km) path. This route provides a longer running experience through two significant parks, featuring lake views and varied parkland. Local runners also love the Lake in Butarque Park loop from Julián Besteiro, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through Butarque Park, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. This moderate route offers a substantial run within a large urban park. Jogging around Leganés is defined by its accessible parks, tranquil lakes, and urban green spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogging routes Leganés to more advanced running trails. The routes in Leganés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,400 runners have used komoot to explore Leganés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Leganés?
A: Leganés offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 70 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy jogs to more challenging runs.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Leganés?
A: Yes, Leganés has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. There are 11 routes classified as easy, often found within the city's parks and green spaces, providing a gentle introduction to jogging in the area.

Q: What are some options for longer distance running in Leganés?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14835520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake in Butarque Park – Las Presillas Park loop from San Nicasio&lt;/a&gt; offer an extensive 11.3-mile (18.1 km) path through two significant parks. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19142248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polvoranca Lake – Polvoranca Park Entrance loop from Parque Polvoranca&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.4 miles (18.4 km) long.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Leganés?
A: Many of the running routes in Leganés are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958016636/parque-de-polvoranca-y-la-laguna-de-maria-pascuala-running-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque de Polvoranca y la Laguna de María Pascuala – running circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.7-mile (6.0 km) loop that explores a park and its lake.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on Leganés running trails?
A: Leganés's running routes are characterized by urban green spaces, tranquil lakes, and well-maintained parks. You can enjoy scenic views, especially around areas like Parque de Polvoranca and Butarque Park, which feature lakes and varied parkland.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Leganés?
A: The running routes in Leganés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the accessible parks, the peaceful lake views, and the variety of terrain suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for advanced runners?
A: Yes, for advanced runners, Leganés offers several difficult routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14835520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake in Butarque Park – Las Presillas Park loop from San Nicasio&lt;/a&gt; is an 11.3-mile (18.1 km) path classified as difficult, providing a more strenuous workout.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Leganés?
A: The best time to go jogging in Leganés is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are mild and pleasant. Summers can be very hot, especially midday, while winters are generally mild but can be chilly.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the running routes?
A: While jogging in Leganés, you're not far from various attractions. You could explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/664165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de Campo Lake&lt;/a&gt; or historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3816969" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Palace of Madrid&lt;/a&gt;, which are within a reasonable distance from Leganés.

Q: Can I find running routes suitable for families in Leganés?
A: Many of the easy and moderate routes within Leganés's parks, such as those in Parque de Polvoranca, are suitable for families. Their relatively flat terrain and well-maintained paths make them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Is public transport accessible to reach jogging routes in Leganés?
A: Leganés is well-connected by public transport, including metro and Cercanías (commuter rail) lines. Many parks and green spaces where running routes are located are within walking distance of public transport stops, making them easily accessible.

Q: Are there options for parking near the jogging trails?
A: As Leganés is an urban area, parking is generally available near its larger parks and green spaces, though it might be paid or require searching for street parking depending on the specific location and time of day.
tips: Nice lake very close to the center of Leganés. It is located within the grounds of the Parque de Polvoranca, a nice place to escape from the city where you can enjoy a variety of paths and paths in good condition to alternate your circuits!

The Polvoranca Park is located across the M-406 and you can easily get there from Leganés or even Alcorcón. It offers everything you need, from fountains, recreational areas, trails and varied paths, bird observatory and you cannot miss visiting the María Pascuala Lake. An ideal place to escape from the city and connect with nature!
